scope: compact routed-MoE W1/W3 xgrad fused with token input-gradient reduction

probe.contract: dgate_compact/dup_compact [assignments, intermediate] x w1/w3 [experts, intermediate, hidden] with direct token reduction -> dX [tokens, hidden]
probe.reason_for_float32_out: The first viability probe tests the direct-reduction contract with FP32 atomic accumulation before deciding whether a production BF16 accumulation path is worth pursuing.

promote: the fused W1/W3 xgrad plus input-reduce tensor contract as a correctness oracle
the standalone S2048 probe for future CK/TileLang comparisons
demote: direct atomic Triton fused xgrad+reduce for DSv4 full-size MoE
FP32 atomic accumulation as a near-term full-layer integration path
decision.reason: The direct atomic Triton kernel is numerically correct at tiny and S2048 shapes, but the S2048 probe is 40.31 ms versus 7.71 ms for padded torch W1/W3 xgrad plus input index_add. The atomic direct-reduction schedule loses the MFMA efficiency and launch shape benefits needed to close the MoE backward gap.
decision.next: Keep the retained compact-reduce padded torch.bmm path as baseline. Future compact-native work should target CK/CK-Tile or TileLang grouped GEMM schedules for tiny per-expert M, and should avoid direct atomics to the token gradient unless a split/reduce design demonstrates higher utilization.
